caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 8122 and includes cemetery/crematory activity, not a pure 812210 funeral-home wage structure.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: No time-and-motion study measures AI-task share in funeral homes; fractions are bounded judgment, not observed automation.
inputs.pi_dist: No source reports realized AI savings pass-through for funeral homes.
inputs.pi_dist: Cremation mix and online price transparency affect revenue economics separately from administrative cost savings.
inputs.pi_moat: No causal evidence located on case-volume retention after a family-owned home is acquired and the selling director exits.
inputs.pi_moat: SCI's scale and broker marketing are not direct evidence for a lower-middle-market independent.
inputs.t50_years: No representative funeral-home AI adoption survey was located.
inputs.t50_years: This is an implementation estimate, not a forecast based on consolidation or owner age.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: This is unsupported bounded judgment and should not be read as a reported market statistic.
inputs.historical_analogs: Cremation substitution is a consumer-preference and service-mix change, not a technological-adoption precedent.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Retirement intent is not an owner-age survey and cannot establish the requested 60-plus percentage.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: The reported survey context and population should be confirmed in diligence before extrapolating to a local acquisition market.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is a floor based on four directly documented buyers, not a census of all acquirers.
inputs.active_consolidators: Active buyer count does not show which buyers will pursue a particular geography, size or ownership structure.
inputs.buy_mult: S11 is broker guidance, not disclosed $1-10M-EBITDA transaction comps.

inputs.buy_mult: The source describes 4.0x-7.0x while also distinguishing SDE for smaller businesses from EBITDA for larger ones; it is imperfect support and requires financial normalization and deal-specific comps.
inputs.buy_mult: Real estate, owner compensation, pre-need liabilities and related-party rents can materially change normalized EBITDA and enterprise value.
inputs.exit_mult: S11 is broker guidance rather than disclosed EBITDA transaction comps in the specified target band.
inputs.exit_mult: The EBITDA/SDE basis issue and local transferability risk make a flat multiple assumption more defensible than a precise exit forecast.
inputs.pricing_structure: NFDA medians are national GPL figures and exclude some cemetery, vault and cash-advance charges; they are not a target-company price list.
inputs.pricing_structure: SCI combines funeral and cemetery contexts and is not a lower-middle-market peer.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $162,073 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 812210); class <5: 4,937 firms, avg revenue/firm $548,492 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $85,839 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 3,060 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,134,884 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $177,609 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 1,786 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,855,167 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $290,334 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 893 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,331,400 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $677,864 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 40 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,374,525 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,875,613 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 9 firms, avg revenue/firm $423,789,444 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $66,323,048 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 40

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI can automate a meaningful fraction of paperwork and routine communications, but licensed, physical and family-facing tasks remain human. The role-weighted estimate deliberately strips proxy grounds labor and limits licensed-role fractions to embedded administration.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) direct: labor compensation (L02) $5,800.0M / sectoral output (T30) $17,793.8M, year 2022

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact identity, frozen digests, dataset injection, arithmetic, finalization, and memo rendering all pass. All eleven registered sources resolve; the ten score-driving sources support the cited facts or the disclosed direction of estimate inputs. The thesis remains useful, and no material defect is likely to change the factor direction or kill conclusion, but the authored scope, proxy, adoption, pass-through, succession, and multiple-basis caveats must remain visible.

sources.title: North American Industry Classification System — 812210 Funeral Homes and Funeral Services
sources.what_it_supports: NAICS boundary: establishments preparing the dead and conducting funerals, including wakes, transportation and caskets; used to distinguish the target from cemetery-only activity.

sources.title: Funeral Service Workers — Occupational Outlook Handbook
sources.what_it_supports: Funeral director and manager duties, administrative components, physical and family-facing service work, supervised training and state licensing requirements.

sources.title: 2024 NFDA Cremation & Burial Report
sources.what_it_supports: Cremation trend and 2023 median GPL charges for burial, cremation and direct cremation; supports the revenue-per-case and terminal-demand risk.

sources.title: Funeral Rule — Federal Trade Commission
sources.what_it_supports: General Price List, consumer choice and itemized-pricing requirements that shape the operator's pricing structure.

sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 Funeral Industry Practices Rule: Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking
sources.what_it_supports: The live policy record on possible online/electronic price disclosures and the consequent transparency risk.

sources.title: Resolve to Get Your Affairs in Order in 2025 — NFDA
sources.what_it_supports: Reported retirement intent, formal succession-plan incidence and disruption-preparedness among funeral-home owners.

sources.title: Service Corporation International 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: SCI's current footprint, 2025 acquisition activity, preneed backlog and comparable funeral revenue, cremation-rate and services-performed disclosures.

sources.title: Foundation Partners Group Launches Next Chapter with New Investment, New Ownership and Bold Plan for Growth
sources.what_it_supports: Foundation Partners' scale, network and acquisition-led growth history.

sources.title: Carriage Services Announces the Acquisition of Osceola Memory Gardens and Funeral Homes
sources.what_it_supports: A recent strategic acquisition of a combination operation, five stand-alone funeral homes, a crematory and a cremation-focused business.

sources.title: Our History — Park Lawn Corporation
sources.what_it_supports: Park Lawn's recent announced U.S. funeral-home and cremation-provider acquisitions.

sources.title: How to Sell Your Funeral Home — John Salony M&A
sources.what_it_supports: Current broker guidance describing a 4.0x-7.0x range and the distinction between SDE for smaller homes and EBITDA for larger businesses; used only as imperfect directional support for flat multiple estimates.

title: Funeral Homes and Funeral Services
